title: How Many Solar Panels Do I Need?
description: The number of panels depends on energy usage, panel wattage, roof space, and local sunlight.
quickAnswer: Most homes need enough panels to offset a meaningful share of annual electricity use, but the exact count depends on your bill and roof conditions.

scenario.title: Panel Count Example
details: Roughly 13-18 modern panels depending on wattage

factors: Annual kWh usage from the utility bill
Local production estimates for the roof plane

faqs.question: Can I size solar from my bill?
faqs.answer: Yes, as a rough starting point. Annual kWh usage from your utility bill is better for final sizing.

faqs.question: Is monthly bill amount enough to size a system?
faqs.answer: Monthly bill amount is useful for a rough estimate, but annual kWh usage is better because rates, fees, and seasonal usage can distort the bill.

faqs.question: Can a smaller system still make sense?
faqs.answer: Yes. A smaller system can make sense when roof space is limited, shade is uneven, or utility rules make exporting excess electricity less valuable.

sources: U.S. Department of Energy homeowner solar resources
DSIRE state and utility incentive database
